forced_mine_count: {minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). How many hidden cells are forced to be mines?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). Count the hidden cells that must contain mines.
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). Determine how many hidden cells are certainly mines.
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s) and nearby flags. How many hidden cells must be mines?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). How many covered cells are guaranteed to contain mines?
forced_safe_count: {minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). How many hidden cells are forced to be safe?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). Count the hidden cells that cannot contain mines.
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). Determine how many hidden cells are certainly safe.
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s) and nearby flags. How many hidden cells must be safe?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell(s). How many covered cells are guaranteed not to contain mines?
forced_mine_cell_label: {minesweeper_rule_text} Among the labeled hidden cells A-D, which one is guaranteed to be a mine?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Which labeled hidden cell A-D must contain a mine?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Using the visible numbers and nearby flags, which labeled covered cell is certainly a mine?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Choose the labeled hidden cell that is forced to be a mine.
{minesweeper_rule_text} Which labeled hidden cell is guaranteed to contain a mine?
remaining_mine_count: {minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell. How many more mines must be adjacent to that marked number?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell. Count the additional adjacent mines still needed by the marked number.
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the outlined opened clue cell and nearby flags. How many more adjacent mines does that number require?
{minesweeper_rule_text} For the outlined opened clue cell, how many adjacent mines are still unaccounted for?
{minesweeper_rule_text} Use the marked opened number cell. How many more mines must touch it?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_open_grid: a Minesweeper grid with opened number cells, hidden cells, and a few flags
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_mixed_grid: a Minesweeper grid with opened number cells, hidden cells, flags, and extra hidden distractor cells
code_prompt_defaults.minesweeper_rule_text: In Minesweeper, each opened number tells how many mines are in its eight neighboring cells, and each flag marks a known mine. If a number already has that many adjacent flags, its other hidden neighbors are safe. If the number still needs exactly all remaining hidden neighbors, those hidden neighbors are mines.
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_forced_mine_count: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1], one for each hidden cell forced to be a mine
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_forced_mine_count: set "answer" to the count of hidden cells that must be mines
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_forced_safe_count: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1], one for each hidden cell forced to be safe
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_forced_safe_count: set "answer" to the count of hidden cells that must be safe
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_forced_mine_cell_label: set "annotation" to the [x, y] pixel point at the center of the labeled hidden cell that is guaranteed to be a mine
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_forced_mine_cell_label: set "answer" to the single option letter printed inside the guaranteed mine cell
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_remaining_mine_count: set "annotation" to the [x, y] pixel point at the center of the marked opened number cell
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_remaining_mine_count: set "answer" to the number of additional mines that must be adjacent to the marked opened number cell
